headline: Cursor hits $50B valuation while Claude Code dominates with $2.5B revenue — agentic coding era is here

stories.title: Cursor seeking $50B valuation, doubles revenue in 3 months
stories.summary: AI coding startup Cursor is in talks for a funding round that would value it at $50B, nearly doubling from its Nov $29.3B valuation. The company hit $2B annualized revenue in Feb, which doubled in just 3 months, fr lowkey insane for a 4-year-old startup.
stories.takeaway: Enterprise adoption is driving sustainable growth. Cursor's shift to 60% enterprise revenue + new cloud agents launching shows agentic coding is moving from side tool to production infrastructure.

stories.title: Cursor launches cloud agents & automations — devs becoming managers of AI factories
stories.summary: Cursor rolled out autonomous cloud agents that run on their own, plus automations that trigger agents via Slack/GitHub events. Agent users now 2x tab users, marking a shift from prompt-and-response to set-and-forget agentic work.
stories.takeaway: The third era of AI coding just arrived. Developers are moving from writing code → directing agents → managing fleets of AI workers. Cursor reports 1/3 of their PRs are now agent-generated.

stories.title: Claude Code hits $2.5B revenue, pushes Anthropic toward $2B+ annual run rate
stories.summary: Anthropic's Claude Code generated $2.5B annualized revenue by Feb 2026, doubling from Nov. Time mag reports Cherny (lead dev) stopped writing code entirely, letting Claude Code handle everything — no human fallback needed anymore.
stories.takeaway: Claude Code Security discovered 500+ vulnerabilities in production codebases that humans missed for decades. This isn't just about speed — it's about finding bugs humans can't see. Anthropic's doubling quotas Mar 13-27 to manage demand surge.

stories.title: GitHub kills free Copilot Pro for 2M students, restricts model selection Mar 12
stories.summary: GitHub rolled out new 'Copilot Student plan' on Mar 12, blocking students from manually selecting Claude Opus, Sonnet, or GPT-5.4 — only Auto mode routing available now. Community forum got 1,836 downvotes. tbh this is kinda rough for learning devs who built workflows around specific models.
stories.takeaway: Frontier models too expensive to give away freely anymore. Even Microsoft-backed platforms are pulling back. Shows the hidden economics of bundled model access are breaking down across the industry.

watchNext.title: Anthropic's Claude Partner Network — $100M investment into enterprise adoption
watchNext.why: Anthropic just announced Claude Certified Architect certifications + partner training program. Signal that Claude is going full enterprise play. This is how coding agents move from novelty to production backbone.

watchNext.title: Cursor ACP in JetBrains IDEs + 30 new plugins from Atlassian, GitLab, Datadog
watchNext.why: Cursor expanded to JetBrains (Mar 4) + launched plugin marketplace with 30+ integrations by Mar 11. Shows agentic coding shifting from standalone tools to full IDE ecosystem. Enterprise will go where IDE tooling lives.
